topic: Verification of c6 H1 unit-citation patch: ±2 µGal → ±1.2 µGal in vise paragraph ensemble-bounded defender pivot (ISS-2070)
change_summary: ISS-2070 patch self-applied 2026-05-17T12:20:14Z by decider-self-apply-patch: replaced "±2 µGal per-station uncertainty bands the dome publishes" (47 chars) with "±1.2 µGal per-station uncertainty bands (propagating the dome's ±2 nT magnetic bands through κ=1.677 nT/µGal)" (109 chars) inside the vise paragraph of SEC-6.14 in data/sections.json:part6.html. Net delta +62 chars; closes c6 H1 moderate (sections.json:part6.html verified by grep).
our_argument_summary: SEC-6.14 vise paragraph argues a two-horned forced choice: either κ applies generally (in which case it predicted a contact-phase pulse at Membach 1999 and the Chinese SG network 2009 — both null), or κ is path-dependent (in which case the August 2026 eclipse prediction must lean on the W004 −22.24 nT baseline rather than BOU −10.9 nT to land station Δg in the 10–13 µGal target band). The defender's best ensemble-bounded pivot — ΔB = 15 nT (Kim & Chang 2018 typical-totality upper edge) → Δg = 8.94 µGal — is 1.06 µGal below the 10 µGal lower target, which the c6 H1 patch concedes is inside the dome's per-station uncertainty band (now correctly cited as ±1.2 µGal, propagated from the dome's published ±2 nT magnetic bands through κ=1.677 nT/µGal). The closing anchor is "Both horns lose at least one WIN."

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Verified per-station uncertainty bands in raw-text/03-predictions.txt lines for E001–E009: Ebro ±2.1 nT, San Pablo ±2.0 nT, Eskdalemuir ±2.1 nT, Lerwick ±1.9 nT, Hartland ±2.6 nT, Niemegk ±1.3 nT, Canary ±1.0 nT, Chambon ±2.0 nT, Coimbra ±2.0 nT. The patched parenthetical's choice of ±2 nT corresponds to the median/modal uncertainty (5 of 9 stations are at exactly ±2.0 nT, 7 of 9 fall in [±1.9, ±2.1] nT). Through κ=1.677 nT/µGal: 2/1.677 = 1.1925 ≈ ±1.2 µGal. Math holds. The 8.94 µGal vs 10 µGal shortfall (1.06 µGal) is inside the ±1.2 µGal band by ~14 nGal, so the ensemble-bounded defender pivot remains live as the patch claims.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Per-station predictions in 03-predictions.txt are static published values with FSF coefficients derived from V12 H(r)/r dome geometry; not live-fetched.
data_sources_used: dome predictions.html / 03-predictions.txt baseline + per-station eclipse predictions
Kim & Chang 2018 typical-totality ensemble (5–15 nT, 207 measurements)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: The H1 patch fix is the parenthetical citation chain — it explicitly shows ±2 nT → κ=1.677 → ±1.2 µGal. This reveals the propagation step, which is a methodological improvement: a defender or skeptic can now audit the propagation rather than accept the ±1.2 µGal number on faith. (Caveat noted under deeper_analysis: the parenthetical does not include κ uncertainty σ_κ in the propagation; in practice this would widen the band slightly and favor the defender, so the omission is conservative on our side.)
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None — patched text matches raw-text/03-predictions.txt published bands within rounding (±2 nT modal, ±1.2 µGal propagated).

code_analysis_tags.note: SEC-6.14 is a section-level review, not per-WIN. The per-WIN tags for the κ-cluster (WIN-013, WIN-014, WIN-058, WIN-058b) live in their own reviews. The aggregate this section relies on is the published per-station uncertainties from raw-text/03-predictions.txt, which the patched parenthetical now correctly cites.

kernel_of_truth.description: The dome's per-station uncertainty bands (raw-text/03-predictions.txt: Ebro ±2.1, San Pablo ±2.0, Hartland ±2.6, etc.) are legitimately published with the per-station predictions and represent a genuine measurement-noise + model-uncertainty estimate. Treating them as a defender's lifeline for marginal shortfalls (here 1.06 µGal < ±1.2 µGal) is methodologically fair — the dome is not being held to a tighter standard than it published (raw-text/03-predictions.txt:E001-E009 published bands).
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The ensemble-bounded defense getting the 8.94 µGal value 'within band' of 10 µGal is the closest plausible defender path, but it requires (a) selecting the upper edge of the Kim & Chang ensemble (15 nT, not 10 nT typical) and (b) selecting modal per-station band (±2 nT, not min ±1.0 nT). Even granting both, the dome still must commit to W004 as the operational baseline rather than BOU, which is the other half of the vise — and W004 is a single-event derivation, not an ensemble-based baseline (sections.json:part6 anchor "Both horns lose at least one WIN" at offset 112698).

text_fingerprint.verdict_anchor: Both horns lose at least one WIN
text_fingerprint.patched_phrase_present: ±1.2 µGal per-station uncertainty bands (propagating the dome's ±2 nT magnetic bands through κ=1.677 nT/µGal)

deeper_analysis:
**Patch verification (sections.json:part6.html offset ~111438, ISS-2070 fix):** The c6 H1 moderate hole flagged a unit-citation error — text claimed "±2 µGal per-station uncertainty bands the dome publishes" but the dome publishes per-station bands in nT (range ±1.0 to ±2.6, raw-text/03-predictions.txt lines E001–E009). The decider self-applied the recommendation (a) replacement: now reads "±1.2 µGal per-station uncertainty bands (propagating the dome's ±2 nT magnetic bands through κ=1.677 nT/µGal)". Math audit: 2/1.677 = 1.1925 → ±1.2 µGal (correct rounding). 1.06 µGal shortfall (10 − 8.94) is inside ±1.2 µGal band by 0.14 µGal. The patch's conclusion — "the ensemble-bounded defense is live and the dome can credibly argue the shortfall disappears under station-level uncertainty" — holds.

**Honest defender critique audit (Advocate Mode):** Two probes I considered raising as new holes and rejected on inspection.

Probe 1 — heterogeneous arithmetic: The paragraph mixes ensemble upper edge (15 nT, best-case ΔB) with per-station uncertainty band (modal ±2 nT). A defender could argue: ensemble values across path stations span more than 15 nT (typical 5–15), so individual-station Δg under typical ensemble values would be 3.0–9.0 µGal, far below 10 µGal. But this attack helps OUR argument, not the defender's: we are reporting the defender's BEST case (8.94 µGal under ensemble upper edge); typical-station Δg would be much worse for the dome. Not a hole.

Probe 2 — κ-uncertainty omission: The parenthetical propagates ±2 nT through κ as if κ were known exactly. Strictly, σ_Δg² = (σ_B/κ)² + (B·σ_κ/κ²)². For typical σ_κ ≈ 0.1 nT/µGal (~6% of κ=1.677), the extra term at ΔB=8.94 nT is 0.32 µGal — bringing the band to ~±1.25 µGal. This widens the defender's room (favors defender), so our omission is conservative on OUR side. Not a hole; if anything a future strengthening could acknowledge the κ-uncertainty correction explicitly.

**Carryover from c6 (deferred-optional):** Two minor c6 holes (H2 "high-coverage central stations" terminology and H3 vise paragraph density) remain unaddressed. Both were explicitly marked "Optional" in the c6 recommendations and the decider correctly did not file open-issues for them (open-issues.json grep on SEC-6.14: 0 matches; closed-issues.json grep: only ISS-2070 H1). Not re-raising them in c7 — re-raising optional holes that the decider deliberately did not action would be churn. If a future change to the vise paragraph creates an opportunity to apply both, that would be the natural time to revisit; absent that, no_change.

**Choice of ±2 nT specifically (defender nitpick consideration):** Per-station bands in raw-text/03-predictions.txt: 5 of 9 stations at exactly ±2.0 nT, 7 of 9 in [±1.9, ±2.1]. The modal/median is ±2 nT, mean ~±1.9. The parenthetical's choice is the modal value and is the natural representative — defensible without further qualification. A pedant could ask for station-specific propagation (Hartland ±2.6 → 1.55 µGal would widen the defender's window further); a different pedant could ask for the minimum ±1.0 → 0.60 µGal would close it. The modal choice is the middle path. Not flagging.

**Net assessment:** Patch landed cleanly, math is correct (verified independently), conclusion holds (1.06 < 1.2 µGal still within band). The vise paragraph's structural two-horned argument remains intact and is now methodologically honest about how the ±1.2 µGal band is derived. recommended_action = no_change.

citations_verified: raw-text/03-predictions.txt per-station uncertainty bands (E001–E009): match patched ±2 nT representative claim (5 of 9 stations at exactly ±2.0 nT)
κ = 1.677 nT/µGal: matches dome-published κ value (consistent across prior c2–c6 reviews)
Kim & Chang 2018 ensemble 5–15 nT typical-totality (207 measurements): cited consistently with prior c4/c5 reviews
Mansinha 2001 Membach SG null at 0.01–0.1 µGal noise floor (J. Geodet. Soc. Japan 47(1) 387–391, DOI 10.11366/sokuchi1954.47.387): cited and verified in prior cycles
citations_unchecked: Section 4.2 cross-reference: not re-validated this cycle (no change since c6)
W004 −22.24 nT empirical baseline derivation: not re-validated this cycle (no change since c6)

summary_for_decider:
c6 H1 ISS-2070 patch verified clean. ±2 µGal → ±1.2 µGal replacement landed exactly as recommendation (a); math holds (2/1.677 = 1.1925 ≈ ±1.2; 1.06 µGal shortfall inside ±1.2 band). 0 critical, 0 major, 0 moderate, 0 minor new holes. Two c6 minor optional holes (H2 station-criterion terminology, H3 vise paragraph density) remain unaddressed but were never opened as ISSes — not re-raising. recommended_action = no_change. Verdict 'Refuted by Data' anchor 'Both horns lose at least one WIN' remains intact. Adversarial Advocate Mode probes (heterogeneous arithmetic, κ-uncertainty omission) evaluated and rejected — neither yields a real attack vector. Carryover signaling: unresolved_prior_cycle = [] (the optional minor holes have no ISSes to carry).

Not batching items 375 (WIN-070 c1, 7 holes — fails gate b ≤2 holes) or 376 (part6.html#p6-610-9 RW-002 integration — class='verification' technically batchable but underlying work is rewrite-integration which the rewrite-verify branch carves out as singleton; defense-in-depth on content-preservation). Both stay singleton for next runs.
